verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN38 homolog, also known as TGN46) encodes a type I transmembrane protein that resides predominantly in the trans-Golgi network (TGN). It continuously cycles between the TGN and the plasma membrane via endosomes, but at steady state the vast majority of the protein is retained in the TGN. The brief transit through the plasma membrane is transient cycling rather than stable surface residence; it is rapidly re-internalized. There is no well-documented stable or wash-resistant surface pool. Its biology is primarily as an endomembrane/TGN-resident sorting protein.
